articles.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
articles.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

articles.title: Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch co-ordinated attacks across Mali
articles.summary: Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north, in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.

articles.title: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
articles.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

articles.title: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
articles.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

articles.title: Rights groups critical as Venezuela prisoner release scheme 'coming to an end'
articles.summary: More than 500 political prisoners are thought to still be in jail, despite the releases since the amnesty law was brought in.

articles.title: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
articles.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

articles.title: Thirty-one sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
articles.summary: Many died due to conditions at a warehouse they'd been shipped to, while others arrived dead, authorities say.

articles.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
articles.summary: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

articles.title: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
articles.summary: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.

articles.title: The viral manifesto of 'anti-woke' tech boss with NHS and defence contracts
articles.summary: The boss of the controversial tech company with UK government contracts published a 22-point plan on the future of the West.
